title: Operators Should Be Used on Compatible Types
description: Operators should be used on types that have a well-defined relationship. For example, comparison operators should be used with operands of the same type.
message: Operator '{{ operator }}' should not be used on '{{ left_type }}' and '{{ right_type }}'.
impact: Code readability and maintainability may be affected.
recommendation: Use appropriate types for comparison operators.
why_is_this_an_issue: Misuse of operators can lead to unexpected results and bugs.
how_to_fix: Ensure that the operands of the operator are of the same type.

logic.new_check_type_doc: Checks if the left and right operands have compatible types based on the operator type.

metadata.title: Operators Should Be Used on Compatible Types
